cases.title: Last 24 hours with local git and validation available
cases.user_request: Review my commits from the last 24 hours and tell me what I probably broke, with the smallest safe fixes.

notes: Git history, diffs, and targeted local validation are available.
The report should rank only evidence-backed findings.

static_checks: Findings cite specific commits or changed files.
Weak signals are separated from fix proposals.
review_questions: Does each proposed issue clearly tie back to one or more recent commits?
Does the report avoid generic cleanup ideas and stay behavior-focused?
Does every proposed remediation include a narrow validation path?

cases.title: Single commit review with a tight write surface
cases.user_request: Inspect commit c9ad52f for likely regressions and suggest the smallest possible follow-up sessions.

notes: The review must stay pinned to one commit.
The user wants fix sessions only if the evidence clears the bar.

artifact_requirements: fix sessions only for evidence-backed issues
static_checks: The output does not drift into other commits or general repo health.
review_questions: Does the analysis remain anchored to the requested commit?
Does it explicitly say when a suspicious signal is too weak for a fix proposal?
Are the follow-up sessions minimal enough to review quickly?

cases.title: Overbroad request that should trigger scope discipline
cases.user_request: Scan everything I changed this month and tell me all the bugs I introduced.

notes: The requested window is broader than the skill's ideal focused review.
A conservative response should narrow the review or clearly state the limitations.

static_checks: Avoids pretending a month-long sweep has the same confidence as a focused pass.
review_questions: Does the response acknowledge the overly broad scope instead of overclaiming confidence?
Does it offer a narrower and more reliable review approach?
Does it avoid speculative bug findings unsupported by concrete evidence?
